attributes.title: Fossil CFO Sunil Doshi to depart, Andrew Skobe named interim
attributes.content:
• Fossil Group (NASDAQ:FOSL) has engaged Ankura Consulting Group, to provide interim-CFO services and has appointed Andrew Skobe as the Co.’s Interim CFO, effective immediately.
  • Skobe assumes the CFO role from Sunil Doshi, who is leaving the Co. for another opportunity.
  • Doshi will remain with the Co. through July 19, 2024, to ensure a smooth transition, and the Co. has initiated a search for a permanent CFO successor to Mr. Doshi.

attributes.title: Teva focus of FTC investigation over inhaler patents
attributes.content:
The FTC has begun an investigation of Teva Pharmaceutical (NYSE:TEVA) over its apparent refusal to withdraw patents related to its asthma and COPD inhalers that the agency argues is delaying generic competition.

The agency has issued a civil investigative demand seeking internal communications and other information related to these patents that are listed in the FDA's Orange Book, according to The Washington Post.

In June. Sen. Elizabeth Warren (D-Mass.) and Rep. Pramila Jayapal (D-Wash.) sent letters to multiple drugmakers, including Teva, asking them to remove "sham patent listings." These letters followed a similar request from the FTC on April 30.

“Our decision to maintain our patent listing comes following serious and thoughtful consideration of the FTC's contentions and our concern for Teva's strict compliance with law," Brian Savage, Teva's general counsel for global litigation, wrote in a letter to the lawmakers reviewed by the Post. "At no time did Teva use these patent listings to raise prices or stifle competition by preventing cheaper generic drugs from entering the market."

In June, a New Jersey federal judge ruled that five patents covering Teva's ProAir HFA asthma inhaler are "improperly listed" in the Orange Book, handing a victory to Amneal Pharmaceuticals (AMRX) which wants to market a generic version.

More on Teva Pharmaceutical

attributes.title: Chewy stock erases gains linked to 'Roaring Kitty' 6.6% stake disclosure
attributes.content:
Chewy (NYSE:CHWY) shares were down ~6% shortly after market open on Monday, erasing early gains it witnessed after influential trader Keith Gill, known online as Roaring Kitty, disclosed a 6.6% stake in the pet food retailer.

Gill disclosed holding of 9M shares in Chewy (CHWY) Class A common stock as of June 24, 2024 in a 13G filing. Shares had jumped as much as 25% premarket in initial reaction to the news.

The stake disclosure comes just days after Gill posted a picture of a dog on social media site X. Gill’s post on Thursday sent Chewy's shares soaring to a near one-year high in intraday trading.

Rival, Petco (WOOF) shares also jumped 6% following the disclosure.

Roaring Kitty has spoken favorably about Chewy (CHWY) founder Ryan Cohen in the past in the context of backing up his bullish thesis on GameStop (GME).

Short interest on Chewy (CHWY) stands at 14.88% of the total float. Last Thursday, Chewy (CHWY) also disclosed that it struck an agreement to repurchase $500M worth of equity directly from its BC Partners.

More on Chewy

attributes.title: Nasdaq, S&P, Dow trade higher as markets enter second half
attributes.content:
The major market averages were higher on Monday, as the markets entered the second half of the year after an encouraging H1.

Early on and the S&P 500 (SP500) was +0.1%, the Dow (DJI) was +0.5%, and the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was near even.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) up 4 basis at 4.44%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 1 basis point to 4.76%. See how Treasury yields have done across the curve at the Seeking Alpha bond page.

U.S. stocks on Friday were not able to hold on to new intraday highs, steadily shedding their gains to eventually end lower. However, Wall Street's benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) closed out the first six months of 2024 with a 14% advance.

The markets had initially rallied on Friday after a core measure of a key inflation gauge logged its lowest Y/Y increase in over three years.

"On the plus side, Q2 saw equities continue to advance, and the S&P 500 hit many more fresh new highs thanks to further gains for the Magnificent 7. But the gains remained narrow, with the equal-weighted S&P 500 actually losing ground in Q2," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

In the holiday-shortened week, traders will be eyeing the June jobs report, which is due on Friday.

"In terms of this week, it will be quite a busy one considering it is a U.S. holiday. Thursday is Independence Day, which means Friday will likely see a skeleton staffing for the latest employment report with the all-important payrolls number," Reid added.

On the economic front today, U.S. PMI Manufacturing inched up less than expected in June. The U.S. PMI Manufacturing Index crept up to 51.6 in June, falling slightly short of the 51.7 consensus level.

Moreover, the ISM Manufacturing PMI slipped further into contraction terrain in June as it edged down to 48.5.

More on markets

  • SPY: Numerous Reasons To Remain Bullish
  • A Look At S&P 500 Sector EPS And Revenue Growth Trends Into Q2 2024
  • 3 Things To Watch Out For In The June 2024 Jobs Report

attributes.title: ISM Manufacturing PMI slips further into contraction terrain in June
attributes.content:
ISM Manufacturing PMI edged down to 48.5 in June, vs. 49.2 expected, from 48.7 in May. With the index below 50, U.S. economic activity in the manufacturing sector contracted for a third consecutive month.

The sector contracted for the 19th time in the last 20 months. Note a reading below 50 signals economic contraction.

"Demand was weak again, output declined, and inputs stayed accommodative," said Timothy R. Fiore, chair of the ISM Manufacturing Business Survey Committee.

Employment: 49.3 vs. 51.1 prior.

Prices: 52.1 vs. 57.0.

New Orders: 49.3 vs. 45.4.

Production: 48.5 vs. 50.2.

Supplier Deliveries: 49.8 vs. 48.9.

Inventories: 45.4 vs. 47.9.

More on the U.S. Economy

  • One Down, One To Go
  • The Soft Landing Lives, For Now
  • U.S. PMI Manufacturing inches up less than expected in June

attributes.title: Weekly ETF flows: Utilities and Financials see the highest inflow
attributes.content:
The world's largest exchange-traded fund by AUM, the SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (NYSEARCA:SPY), witnessed a net inflow of $16.7B in the final week of June. 3 out of 4 weeks in June saw an outflow.

Overall, the SPY fund (SPY) wrapped up an outflow of $9.6B for June, climbing 3.53% to ~$544.22, with almost $541B in AUM.

Last week:

5 out of 11 S&P 500 ETFs recorded outflows. The Technology Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLK) saw the highest outflow of ~$8.6B, mainly due to the rebalancing. The second-highest outflow was seen in the Communications Services Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLC) at ~$653.8M, according to data from ETF.com.

The Technology Fund (XLK) and Communication Services Fund (XLC) traded +0.12% and +0.65%, respectively, last week.

The Financial Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLF) recorded the highest inflow of $845.3M last week, followed by the Utilities Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLU) with a $330.1M inflow. These were the two funds that saw the highest outflow for the week-ending June 21.

The financial fund (XLF) and utilities fund (XLU) traded -0.72% and -1.4%, respectively, last week.

June wrap-up

The sector-based funds that saw the highest inflows in June are the utilities fund (XLU) and the financial fund (XLF), syncing with last week's trends. (XLU) and (XLF) bagged an inflow of ~$780M and ~$597M, respectively, in June.

Utilities (XLU) saw inflows for 3 out of 4 weeks in June, while Financial (XLF) bagged inflows only in the final week of June.

The highest outflows in June were recorded in the Consumer Discretionary Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLY) and the Industrial Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLI), $120.45M and $39.39M, respectively.

This week's top funds (in terms of inflows), Utilities (XLU), and Financials (XLF) get Seeking Alpha's Quant rating of Sell and Buy with a score of 2.46 and 3.93, respectively.

More on SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust

  • Top S&P 500 gainers and losers of Q2

attributes.title: Construction spending rises much lesser than expected
attributes.content:
• May Construction Spending: +0.1% M/M to $2,139.8B vs. +0.3% expected and -0.1% in April. The May figure is 6.4 percent above the May 2023 estimate of $2,011.8 billion
  • Spending on private construction was at a seasonally adjusted annual rate of $1,652.1 billion, 0.3 percent below the revised April estimate of $1,656.7 billion.
  • Residential construction was at a seasonally adjusted annual rate of $918.2 billion in May, 0.2 percent below the revised April estimate of $920.3 billion.
  • Nonresidential construction was at a seasonally adjusted annual rate of $733.9 billion in May, 0.3 percent below the revised April estimate of $736.5 billion.
  • The estimated seasonally adjusted annual rate of public construction spending was $487.6 billion, 0.5 percent above the revised April estimate of $485.4 billion, the U.S. Census Bureau said.

attributes.title: Character.AI held talks with Google, Meta, xAI about tie ups: report
attributes.content:
• Artificial intelligence startup Character.AI held talks with Google (NASDAQ:GOOG) (NASDAQ:GOOGL) and Elon Musk's xAI about research partnerships, The Information reported.
  • Character.AI, which was founded by former Google employees, has raised more than $150M in funding from venture capital firms, such as Andreessen Horowitz, to create AI chatbots that encompass anime characters, game character and more. Last week, the company introduced its Character call feature, which the company describes as having characters "ready to answer your call to amplify the fun and help you do whatever it is you want to do."
  • If Character.AI were to do a deal with either Google or xAI or another AI company, Character.AI could access its partners' computing resources and share intellectual property in return, the news outlet added, citing a person familiar with the matter.
  • A research partnership between itself and Meta Platforms (NASDAQ:META) has also been discussed, the news outlet added.
  • Google, Meta and Character.AI did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

attributes.title: Maersk pulls out of bidding for Germany's DB Schenker
attributes.content:
A.P. Moller-Maersk (OTCPK:AMKBY) (OTCPK:AMKBF) +5.9% in Copenhagen trading Monday after saying it withdrew from bidding for German logistics provider DB Schenker, the logistics unit of state-run railway operator Deutsche Bahn that has been reported to be valued at more than €15B ($16.1B).

Maersk (OTCPK:AMKBY) (OTCPK:AMKBF) CEO Vincent Clerc said the company decided to withdraw from the sales process after carrying out a review into the logistics business, which "identified areas of challenges from an integration perspective and ultimately, we concluded that acquiring DB Schenker would not be the right thing to do for our business at this time."

Deutsche Bahn started looking at plans to sell off DB Schenker in March, with a view to raising up to €15B to help pay down its €30B in debts, according to Reuters.

Analysts had questioned the financial logic of a deal, with Jyske Markets saying earlier this year that a deal was unlikely due to limited opportunities to create synergies.

attributes.title: Biggest stock movers today: Crypto stocks, CHWY, SPR, NIO and more
attributes.content:
Stock futures kicked off Q3 and the second half of 2024 on a bullish note, inching up slightly on Monday morning.

Here are some of Monday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Spirit AeroSystems (NYSE:SPR) shares soared over 8% after Boeing (NYSE:BA) announced it would acquire the company in an all-stock deal at $37.25 per share, with a total transaction value of approximately $8.3B, which includes Spirit's debt. This represents a premium of 26% over Spirit's closing stock price on February 29, the day before the acquisition talks were confirmed.
  • Cryptocurrency-related stocks experienced gains on Monday as Bitcoin (BTC-USD) rose over 2%. Bit Digital (NASDAQ:BTBT) surged by 6%, Cleanspark (NASDAQ:CLSK) by 4%, Cipher Mining (NASDAQ:CIFR) by 6%, Coinbase (NASDAQ:COIN) by 3%, Riot Platforms (NASDAQ:RIOT) by 3.4%, Marathon Digital (NASDAQ:MARA) by 6%, and MicroStrategy (NASDAQ:MSTR) by 2.3%.
  • Li Auto's (NASDAQ:LI) shares rose more than 4% after announcing the delivery of 47,774 vehicles in June, a 46.7% increase Y/Y and 36.4% M/M. This momentum continued throughout Q2, with deliveries reaching 108,581, reflecting a 25.5% Y/Y growth. As of June 30, 2024, Li Auto's cumulative deliveries reached 822,345 vehicles, ranking first among China's emerging new energy auto brands. The company has reclaimed the top spot in sales among emerging new energy auto brands since the second quarter, driven by the launch of its new model, Li L6, and improved store efficiency.
  • NIO (NYSE:NIO) gained 5% after releasing its monthly deliveries report, which showed 98% Y/Y growth for the month of June and a nearly 144% increase in Q2 deliveries. The company's cumulative deliveries reached 537,020 as of June 30, 2024.
  • Online pet retailer Chewy's (NYSE:CHWY) shares jumped 25% after stock influencer Keith Gill revealed a 6.6% stake in the company, a holding of ~9M shares in Chewy Class A common stock as of June 24 in 13G Fililng. Gill's cryptic social media post featuring a picture of a dog led to a surge in Chewy's shares to nearly a one-year high. Gill, also known as "Roaring Kitty," played a pivotal role in the 2021 meme-stock frenzy by sharing a screenshot on Reddit showing his substantial position in GameStop stock and options.

More on related stocks:

  • Boeing: Free Cash Flow In Focus Ahead Of Earnings, Weak Momentum

  • Li Auto sales surged 46.7% Y/Y in June, topping China's NEV market

attributes.title: Birkenstock shakes off footwear sector slump after UBS and Citi point to upside
attributes.content:
Birkenstock Holding plc (NYSE:BIRK) gained in early trading on Monday after both UBS and Citi weighed in positively on the footwear stock.

UBS upgraded Birkenstock (BIRK) to a Buy rating from Neutral and hiked its price target to $63. The firm said its bullish view on BIRK is based on confidence that it can achieve stronger sales and margins over the long term. Notably, Birkenstock (BIRK) is believed to be successfully executing its direct-to-consumer expansion strategy better than it expected. UBS forecasts average selling prices for Birkenstock (BIRK) will be stronger than anticipated. Due to the recent momentum, UBS raised its five-year EPS CAGR forecast to 25% from 21% and now has a 2026 EPS estimate on BIRK that is 10% above the consensus estimate.

Meanwhile, Citi resumed coverage on Birkenstock (BIRK) with a Buy rating and price target of $65. Analyst Paul Lejeuz pointed out that BIRK is one of the fastest-growing companies in retail and has industry-leading EBIT margins. "At only €1.5BN ($1.6BN) in F23 sales, BIRK has a significant runway for further growth that supports mid- to high-teens sales growth and a 20%+ EPS CAGR over the next three years," he highlighted.

Shares of Birkenstock (BIRK) were up 1.96% in early trading on Monday. Birkenstock (BIRK) fell last week along with other footwear names after Nike's (NKE) soft guidance rattled the sector. Nike (NKE) warned that sales will decline 10% in its current quarter due to uneven consumer trends and will be down at a mid single-digit clip for the full year. "Nike’s 4Q report indicated its fundamental trends are much worse than we realized. Our key conclusion is there will be no quick rebound for Nike’s earnings," highlighted analyst Jay Sole about the report.

More on Birkenstock Holding

attributes.title: Adecoagro upgraded at J.P. Morgan but disconnect on prices keeps bank sidelined
attributes.content:
With limited downside to sugar and ethanol prices coupled with a weaker Brazilian real, J.P. Morgan upgraded agro-industrial Adecoagro (NYSE:AGRO) to Neutral from Underweight but is remaining on the sidelines given the lack of triggers for higher sugar prices.

J.P. Morgan’s LatAm Food & Beverages and Agribusiness team led by Lucas Ferriera see supply and production issues in the sugar market that should warrant higher prices. But there seems to be a “disconnect” between fundamentals and prices given forecasts for a global deficit ahead tied to drought conditions in Brazil. Sugar prices could increase following the slump of the second half of this year, but non-commercial investors continue to hold a historically low net short position in sugar futures, reflecting more technical bearish sentiment on prices, Ferriera notes.

“Despite the bullish longer-term outlook, it is likely that sugar prices will take their time to recover the ground they lost, given the lack of more near-term triggers,” Ferriera says.

As for ethanol, while current prices face challenges from oversupply and consumer behavior, regulatory changes and weather-related production factors could drive “significant” price movements towards parity with gasoline, J.P. Morgan says.

But despite the bullish outlook for ethanol prices, Adecoagro (AGRO) isn’t “out of the woods yet” given lingering headwinds from the recent weakness in sugar and ethanol prices, which should take Adecoagro (AGRO) some time to bounce back from.

In addition to the upgrade, Ferriera lifted the price target by $1 to $11.50 but lowered his FY24.25 EBITDA by 5% to $456M, 16% below the street consensus.

Adecoagro (AGRO) shares are up 4.5% at Monday’s open and challenging resistance at the 50-day moving average for the first time since mid-May.

More on Adecoagro

  • Adecoagro's FY24 Is Challenged By Sugar Prices, And The Company Is Still A Hold

  • Adecoagro S.A. 2024 Q1 - Results - Earnings Call Presentation

attributes.title: Meta's content, monetization see 20% influence from gen AI - Needham; Raymond ups price target
attributes.content:
Large language models, or LLMs, already influence around 20% of Meta Platforms'(NASDAQ:META) content generation and monetization efforts, Needham noted citing some former employees of the social media company.

Generative AI, or GenAI, today is already driving transformative advertising capabilities at Meta including the Advantage+ product, said former employees of Meta as per conversations with analysts led by Laura Martin at Needham.

The analysts had conversations with former Meta employees and a social media ad agency CEO.

"Over the next 2-3 years, he expects Meta to grow its GPU investments as it integrates generative AI across its entire monetization stack," the analysts noted.

Meta's projected CapEx for 2024 is $36B, with 70%-80% ($25-$28B) allocated to GPU-related investments. Of this, he expects $22B to $25B to be spent on GPUs themselves. He believes Meta will buy more than 150,000 GPUs from Nvidia in 2024. In the near term, the largest share of GPU investments will relate to monetization, with extra GPUs bought to handle search and AI workflows, each accounting for 3%-5% of the total, he estimates, the analysts added as per their conversations.

The analysts noted, as per their conversations, that the main challenge for Facebook and Instagram is slowing user growth. Facebook is not attracting younger users, as they prefer platforms like TikTok, owned by (BDNCE) and Snapchat (SNAP). Even Instagram is struggling to attract young audiences. This is in part due to TikTok's rapid growth, which has surpassed YouTube in impression growth.

While user growth is challenged, user engagement on Facebook and Instagram remains strong. He estimates between 35-50 minutes/day. Meta is currently focused on enhancing its recommendation engine and introducing short-form videos to increase its user engagement lengths, according to the analysts' discussions.

Needham has an Underperform rating on Meta's stock.

Martin and her team noted that they recommend investors to use Meta as a source of funds because — why to buy Meta's stock now, when management is saying that they are in a two to three year investment cycle, and revenue upside will come in three-to-four years.

The analysts worry that Chinese discount retailers like Temu, owned by PDD (PDD), and Shein, which accounted for more than 20% of Meta's 2023 ad revenue, will slow as the geopolitical tensions between the U.S. and China over a potential TikTok ban increase.

The analysts are concerned that that expenses and CapEX estimates will increase faster than consensus estimates during 2024.

They worry that Meta's growing investments in LLMs and in the Metaverse in the near term will lower its return on invested capital, or ROIC, and recommended investors use Meta as a source of funds.

Separately, Raymond James reiterated its Strong Buy rating on Meta and raised the price target on the shares to $600 from $550.

Analysts at Raymond James raised their 2025 CapEX estimates for Meta near Street's high of $50B, which places a modest degree of gross margin pressure (every incremental $10B a ~125bps run-rate gross margin headwind).

The analysts assume the nearly 350K H100 footprint covers the LLM Llama 4 but factor in a '25 Llama 5 B200 build.

Raymond James lowered its '25 EPS estimate by 3% to $24.26 steered mainly by higher infrastructure costs.

The analysts raised their price target to $600 to reflect their bullish stance on GenAI monetization across Llama and consumer/business ad opportunities on the platform.

They reiterated their Strong Buy rating as the GenAI nearly $40B monetization opportunity is underappreciated.

The analysts believe Llama/open-source strategy is well positioned in enterprise and some combo of business and/or consumer AI improve ad monetization with minimal core social business risk.

Meta (META) has a Hold rating at Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating system, which consistently beats the market. Meanwhile, the Seeking Alpha authors' average rating is more positive with a Buy and so is the average Wall Street analysts' rating, Buy.

More on Meta

  • Meta Platforms: Already On Its Way Back To All-Time Highs (Upgrade)
  • Mea Culpa: I Was Wrong About Meta, But I'll Correct That Now

attributes.title: Apple lost share in US, China, UBS says
attributes.content:
Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) lost smartphone market share in China in May despite "deep" discounting, investment firm UBS said, citing data from Counterpoint Research.

Shares rose 1.2% in early trading on Monday.

Cupertino, Calif.-based Apple ended the month with 11% of the smartphone market, even as iPhone units rose 0.9% year-over-year, analyst David Vogt wrote in an investor note. "We estimate iPhone sell-through in China was largely flat YoY during May-24 in a market that grew 11% YoY," Vogt wrote. "While Apple bulls may note the data is backwards looking and is not likely indicative of Apple's AI smartphone opportunity next year, we note that iPhone share loss to Huawei and other Chinese OEMs acts as a material governor on iPhone unit growth."

Vogt, who has a Neutral rating and $190 price target on Apple, also pointed out that iPhone units in the U.S. were down 16% year-over-year in a market that was down roughly 10%, indicating the iPhone lost share in the U.S. as well.

Indian iPhone units also fell roughly 7% year-over-year, as the market declined 7% year-over-year, Vogt said.

Conversely, iPhone units in Europe rose 19.5% year-over-year, albeit in a market that rose 21%, Vogt added.

More on Apple

  • Apple: Upgrading To Buy On New Catalyst And Adjusted Smartphone Expectations
  • Apple: No Longer A Growth Story
  • Apple Nears Our Target Price (Rating Downgrade)
  • AI to help spur 15% additional gain in tech stocks in second-half: Wedbush

attributes.title: U.S. PMI Manufacturing inches up less than expected in June
attributes.content:
The U.S. PMI Manufacturing Index crept up to 51.6 in June, falling slightly short of the 51.7 consensus, from 51.3 in May.

New orders advanced for a second straight month, though client demand remained muted and business confidence reached a 19-month low.

The "survey shows US manufacturers struggling to achieve strong production growth in June, hamstrung by weak demand from domestic and export markets alike. Although the PMI has now been in positive territory in five of the first six months of 2024, up from just one positive month in 2023, growth momentum remains frustratingly weak," said Chris Williamson, chief business economist at S&P Global Market Intelligence.

More on the U.S. Economy

  • Federal Reserve Watch: There Is Still Plenty Of Money Around

attributes.title: Arthur J. Gallagher acquires Zayla Partners
attributes.content:
• Arthur J. Gallagher & Co (NYSE:AJG) Monday announced the acquisition of Katy, Texas-based Zayla Partners.
  • Terms of the transaction were not disclosed.
  • Zayla is an executive compensation strategy firm that serves clients across various sizes and industries.
  • Chris Crawford, Ian Keas and their team will remain in their current locations in Texas and Colorado under the direction of Steve Coco, Global Human Resources & Compensation Consulting Managing Director for Gallagher's employee benefits consulting and brokerage operations.
  • Source: Press Release

  • Arthur J. Gallagher in advanced talks on THB Chile deal - report

attributes.title: Mama’s Creations joins Russellll 2000 and Russell 3000 indexes
attributes.content:
• Mama’s Creations (NASDAQ:MAMA) Monday announced that the company’s common stock was added to the Russell 2000 and Russell 3000 Indexes after the market closed on June 28, 2024.

attributes.title: Avantor announces J.T. Baker Cell Lysis Solution and J.T. Baker Endonuclease
attributes.content:
• Avantor (NYSE:AVTR) on Monday announced the launch of two products that optimize the gene therapy harvest process - J.T. Baker Cell Lysis Solution and J.T. Baker Endonuclease.
  • The company said that pairing J.T. Baker Cell Lysis Solution with J.T. Baker Endonuclease can further reduce the number of steps at harvest.
  • Press release.

attributes.title: Trump Media joins Russell 1000, Russell 3000 indexes
attributes.content:
• As part of the 2024 annual reconstitution of the Russell indexes, Trump Media & Technology Group (NASDAQ:DJT) has been selected to be included in the Russell 1000 and broad-market Russell 3000 Indexes effective after the U.S. market closed on June 28, 2024.
  • Shares of the media firm, which is majority-owned by former U.S. president Donald Trump, were up around 2% in early trading on Monday, but opened trading with a ~5% dip.
